summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on Zoning Case CC-2023-005, which involved a series of text amendments to the township zoning code. The amendments addressed administrative relief authority for the zoning inspector, barns/storage structures as permitted uses in R1 districts, flag/panhandle lot regulations aligned with Clermont County standards, accessory structures on adjacent lots under common ownership, and new definitions for agricultural terms. Significant discussion arose from a resident concerned about non-conforming status of existing properties in platted subdivisions. The commission voted to approve the proposed text amendments, which will next go before the Board of Trustees on August 8.
decisions: Approved meeting minutes from the May 2, 2023 meeting
Approved proposed zoning text amendments (CC-2023-005) with one abstention (Mr. Curry)
action_items: Text amendments to be presented to the Board of Trustees on August 8, 2023
Next Zoning Commission meeting scheduled for Tuesday, September 3, 2023
key_amounts: 25 feet minimum width for flag lot frontage
500 feet maximum length for flag lot access strip
2 acres minimum lot size for barns/storage structures as principal use in R1
75 feet minimum setback from road for barns/storage structures
10% administrative relief threshold regardless of zoning district
notable_topics: Administrative relief authority for zoning inspector and zoning supervisor
Barns and storage structures as permitted principal use in R1 district
Flag/panhandle lot regulations aligned with Clermont County standards
Accessory structures on adjacent lots under common ownership (Section 7.1B)
Detached accessory structures allowed in front yard on large R1 lots (Section 7.1C)
Definitions added for agricultural building, agriculture, banner, and barn/storage structure
Non-conforming property concerns from resident in platted subdivision
Clermont County Planning Commission unanimously recommended approval
notes: A resident expressed significant frustration about being placed in non-conforming status due to rule changes over the years, particularly regarding a pole barn on a separate parcel in a platted subdivision and a house built across two parcels. The resident purchased the property in 1994 and received proper permits at the time. Mr. Curry abstained from the vote, stating he wanted to 'stay out of that.' The meeting was identified as a Zoning Commission meeting (not Board of Trustees), though the transcript initially referenced 'Board of Entrepreneurs' which appears to be a transcription error. The text amendments were initiated by the trustees after requesting small edits from a previous version reviewed months earlier.
title: ZC 8-1-23
